text:
The paper titled "Multiple-Choice Prophet Inequalities" exists, but it is authored by **Brian Chen, David R. Karger, and Aleksander Madry** (2016), not by "Robert Kleinberg, Sergei M. Krakovski" as cited. The referenced venue (SODA 2005) and authors are completely wrong. The real paper with this exact title is a 2016 student project/report, not a SODA 2005 paper. The name "Sergei M. Krakovski" does not appear in any relevant results. The combination of wrong authors (none match) and wrong venue/year is a hallucination pattern — the cited reference is fabricated.

VERDICT: LIKELY
EXPLANATION: A paper titled "Multiple-Choice Prophet Inequalities" exists, but it is authored by Brian Chen, David R. Karger, and Aleksander Madry (2016), not by "Robert Kleinberg, Sergei M. Krakovski" at SODA 2005. None of the cited authors match, and no paper by Kleinberg and "Krakovski" with this title was found anywhere.
LINK: https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Multiple-Choice-Prophet-Inequalities-Chen-Karger/242b08107d5582fd1ccdf9975492c12ba383a3bd
